stories.title: Giannis Trade — 2 Days to Draft, Blazers Out, Bucks Pursuing More Capital
stories.angle: Two days until the NBA Draft (June 23 at Barclays Center). The Giannis Antetokounmpo trade race has narrowed further: Portland Trail Blazers are "no longer" a likely landing spot, leaving Heat vs. Celtics as the final two. The Bucks are simultaneously pursuing additional first-round draft capital beyond their No. 10 pick, signaling to rival teams they want to "trade up in the lottery if possible." Miami's package (Herro + Kel'el Ware + Jaquez + No. 13 pick) remains the frontrunner. Giannis would agree to a long-term extension with either Miami or Boston.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Giannis trade update: Blazers officially OUT. Final two: Heat vs. Celtics. Bucks chasing more lottery picks on top of No. 10. Marc Stein: deal "drawing near." 48 hours to draft night. #Bucks #NBA

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Giannis will sign a long-term extension with Heat OR Celtics — not a rental. Miami leads with Herro + Ware + Jaquez + No. 13. Celtics counter with Jaylen Brown + picks. Deal expected by draft night. #Bucks #Giannis

article.headline: Giannis Trade Endgame: Blazers Out, Heat vs. Celtics With 48 Hours to Go

research.facts:
Giannis Trade — 2 Days to Draft, Blazers Out, Bucks Pursuing More Capital
**Key Facts (Verified):**
- NBA Draft is June 23-24 at Barclays Center, Brooklyn — 2 days away [HIGH]
- Portland Trail Blazers are "no longer" a likely Giannis landing spot per Bleacher Report [HIGH]
- Final two destinations: Miami Heat and Boston Celtics [HIGH]
- Miami's package: Tyler Herro + Kel'el Ware + Jaime Jaquez Jr. + No. 13 pick [HIGH - multiple sources]
- Celtics' package centers on Jaylen Brown + picks [HIGH]
- Giannis would sign a long-term extension with either Miami or Boston [HIGH]
- Marc Stein reported trade is "drawing near" [MEDIUM]
- Bucks pursuing "additional first-round pick" beyond their No. 10 selection [HIGH - multiple sources]
- Bucks have "left some opposing front offices with the impression they would like to trade up in the lottery if possible" [MEDIUM]
- Bucks "signaling to rival teams and agents alike" they plan to have extra first-round pick [HIGH]
**Sources:** Bleacher Report, ESPN, FanSided, ClutchPoints, Yardbarker

stories.title: NBA Draft Bombshell — Wizards Now "Torn" Between Dybantsa and Peterson
stories.angle: Two days to draft night, the consensus is cracking. Washington Wizards have "increased consideration" on picking Darryn Peterson over AJ Dybantsa at No. 1. ESPN insiders call it a "tough call." Strategic move by Peterson: he only visited Washington and refused meetings with other teams — signaling he wants to be the No. 1 pick and go nowhere else. Dybantsa meanwhile confirmed he didn't work out for any team ("I don't gotta work out. They know what I can do."). Draft June 23-24 at Barclays Center, Brooklyn.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: BREAKING: The No. 1 pick is NOT locked. Wizards now "torn" between Dybantsa and Peterson — ESPN calls it a "tough call." Peterson visited ONLY Washington. Dybantsa skipped all workouts. 2 days. #NBADraft #WashingtonWizards

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Darryn Peterson's move: visit ONLY the Wizards, refuse all other meetings. He's telling Washington: "Take me No. 1 or bust." Two days until draft night at Barclays. Bold. #NBADraft

article.headline: NBA Draft 2026: Wizards "Torn" Between Dybantsa and Peterson With Two Days to Go

research.facts:
NBA Draft Bombshell — Wizards Now "Torn" Between Dybantsa and Peterson
**Key Facts (Verified):**
- Draft date: June 23-24, Barclays Center, Brooklyn [HIGH]
- Wizards won the 2026 NBA Draft Lottery — hold No. 1 pick after 17-65 season [HIGH]
- Washington Wizards have "increased consideration" on Darryn Peterson over AJ Dybantsa [HIGH - Yahoo Sports, Heavy.com]
- ESPN insiders call the decision a "tough call" [HIGH]
- Darryn Peterson visited ONLY the Wizards; refused meetings with all other teams [HIGH]
- AJ Dybantsa revealed he did NOT work out for any team before the draft [HIGH]
- Dybantsa quote: "I didn't work out for anyone; I just visited the cities. Take me to the facility, take me to dinner, talk. I don't gotta work out. They know what I can do." [HIGH - ClutchPoints/Yahoo Sports direct quote]
- Dybantsa stats at BYU: 25.5 PPG, 6.8 RPG, 3.7 APG in 35 games, 51% FG [HIGH]
- Peterson stats at Kansas: 20.2 PPG, missed 11 games due to injury [HIGH]
- Peterson health concerns: cramping issues linked to creatine use per his representatives [MEDIUM]
- Shams Charania: only Wizards granted access to visit Peterson before draft [HIGH]
**Sources:** ClutchPoints, Yahoo Sports, Heavy.com, On3, TotalProSports, Yardbarker

stories.title: Austin Reaves — Lakers in Exclusive Window as Free Agency Clock Ticks
stories.angle: The Lakers are in their exclusive negotiating window with Austin Reaves before free agency opens June 30. Reaves must decline his $14.9M player option by June 29. Lakers can offer 5yr/$239M max. Multiple league sources still expect re-signing, though Nets remain interested at 4yr/$178.5M. Reaves averaged 23.3 PPG last season — decision "will not come down solely to a dollar figure."

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Lakers hold exclusive negotiating window for Reaves before June 30. He can get 5yr/$239M from LA vs. 4yr/$178.5M from the Nets. Decision reportedly "won't come down solely to a dollar figure." #LakeShow #AustinReaves

article.headline: Austin Reaves Free Agency: Lakers in Exclusive Window With 9 Days to Decide

stories.title: Knicks' Repeat Blueprint — Brunson Extension, OG, and the No. 17 Pick
stories.angle: New York is formally out of the Giannis race, pivoting to defending the title. The three pillars of the offseason: (1) Jalen Brunson extension negotiations, (2) OG Anunoby and Karl-Anthony Towns re-signing, (3) No. 17 pick in two days. Free agency opens June 30. The Knicks face a luxury-tax crunch but have the championship goodwill and a well-built core.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: Knicks offseason checklist: Brunson extension, OG Anunoby re-sign, KAT re-sign, No. 17 pick on Thursday. Officially out of Giannis race. Defending champions get to work. #NewYorkKnicks #HoopHeroes

article.headline: Knicks' Repeat Blueprint: Brunson Extension, OG, KAT, and a No. 17 Pick

stories.title: Ja Morant Trade Market — Memphis Holds While NBA Waits on Giannis
stories.angle: Memphis Grizzlies are deliberately pausing Ja Morant trade talks until Giannis resolves on draft night (June 23). Heat remain interested in Morant as Plan B. Minnesota also eyeing Morant. Morant averaged 24.4 PPG last season. Memphis holds the No. 3 pick — could be packaged in a Morant deal or used independently. The post-draft trade market could be explosive.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Memphis won't open the Ja Morant trade market until Giannis resolves on June 23. Heat are Plan B. Minnesota also interested. Once draft night ends, the second domino falls. #Grizzlies #NBA

article.headline: Ja Morant Trade Market: Memphis Is Waiting on Giannis, and Tuesday Night Changes Everything
